user_query:
I need you to transform our small backlog into a dependency-aware task board using the local task management CLI in this workspace. Read input/backlog.csv and do the following:

1) Create parent tasks (one per epic) and child tasks (one per subtask) with the priorities given in the CSV. Use the CSV order to set a linear dependency chain among each epic’s subtasks (subtask 2 depends on subtask 1, etc.). Also create a final integration task for each epic named "QA & Integration Sign-off for <Epic>" that depends on all of that epic’s subtasks.

2) Mark each integration task as blocked with a short reason until all subtasks are completed.

3) Simulate two workers by using the CLI’s atomic claim flow: run task claim --agent=alpha once and task claim --agent=beta once to acquire the two highest-priority unblocked subtasks. Add a brief note to each claimed task with context about what to do next. Complete exactly one of the claimed subtasks (alpha’s is fine), leaving the other in progress.

4) Export the entire task list to JSON using the CLI and save it to output/tasks.json. Make sure this export includes IDs, request_text, status, priority, parent_id, assignee, and notes fields if available.

5) Copy the backlog you used into output/backlog_copy.csv so the export and structure can be cross-checked later.

6) Write a concise runbook to output/runbook.md that explains your plan, the dependency setup per epic, the exact CLI commands you ran (in the order you ran them), and next steps. Include a short risks/assumptions section.

7) Write output/assignment_summary.md listing the currently claimed tasks per agent (alpha and beta) as a simple markdown list with task IDs and titles.

Paths to use:
- Read: input/backlog.csv
- Write: output/tasks.json, output/backlog_copy.csv, output/runbook.md, output/assignment_summary.md

Important working guidelines:
- Don’t pick tasks blindly; use the claim command for exclusive assignment.
- Keep statuses and notes accurate and up to date.
- Use dependency links so integration tasks can only proceed after all subtasks are done.
- Do not store any secrets in notes.

Deliverables are the four files under output/ listed above. If anything in the CSV looks ambiguous, make reasonable assumptions and document them in runbook.md.
